Japanese: １２月３０日　午前　９時４１分 地方裁判所　被告人第５控え室
English: December 30, 9:41 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 5

Japanese: マックスさん、 おはようございます！
English: Good morning, Max.

Japanese: やや、やあ‥‥ハニイたち。
English: Oh... Yeah... Good morning, sweeties...

Japanese: なんか、元気ないですね。
English: You don't seem like you're usual sparkling self today.

Japanese: ほ、本番前は、いつもこうだ。 気にしないでくれたまえ。
English: I'm always like this before I go in front of an audience... I'm working up to it.

Japanese: ‥‥ふふふ。
English: Tee hee...

Japanese: キンチョーしちゃダメだよ マックス。‥‥はい、牛乳。
English: Don't get nervous, Maxy... Here, have a glass of milk.

Japanese: み、ミリカちゃん！
English: R-Regina!!

Japanese: おー、ゴージャス！ マイ・スウィート・ハニイ！
English: How fabulous!! My sweetie pie!! My sweetie pie princess!!

Japanese: 今日は、ボクのステージを見に？
English: You came to watch my performance today?

Japanese: うん！
English: Of course I did!

Japanese: トミーさんがね。 見ておいたほうがいいから、って。
English: Moe told me that I should come and watch this.

Japanese: （トミーさんが‥‥？）
English: (Moe said that...?)

Japanese: ねえ。今日はどんなステージなの？ ヤッパリ、最後は飛ぶのかなあ。
English: So, what kind of performance will you put on today? Lemme guess, you'll fly at the end?
Uh...

Japanese: いや、その‥‥ なあ、ハニイたち。
English: It's not that kind of show... Isn't that right, my sweeties?

Japanese: はい？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ボクのスウィート・ハニイは もしかして‥‥？
English: I think my sweetie pie princess doesn't...

Japanese: 知らないみたいですね。 ‥‥ここがどういう場所なのか。
English: Yeah. She doesn't seem to realize what's going on... Or even where she is...

Japanese: ジーザス‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あ。そろそろ開演時間だ。
English: Well, Max, it looks like it's time to raise the curtain.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、あとでね。 ミリカ、今日はお客さまだから。
English: I'll see you later. Today I'm just a member of the audience!

Japanese: ゴ、ゴージャス！ ‥‥楽しんでくれたまえ‥‥
English: F-Fabulous! Enjoy yourself out there!!

Japanese: がんばってね。マックス！
English: Good luck Max! You're the best!!

Japanese: ミリカちゃん、 ‥‥やっぱり変わってるね。
English: Regina's different, don't you think, Nick?

Japanese: ぐっもおにん！
English: Top of the morning to ya!

Japanese: ‥‥みなさん、 おはようサンバ！
English: Everybody... Let's get ready to get stuck in legal limbo! How low can you go?

Japanese: と‥‥トミーさん。
English: M-Moe.

Japanese: おはようサンバ！
English: Top of the morning to ya, guv'ner.

Japanese: ‥‥お、おはようサンバ。
English: Uhh... Top of the morning.

Japanese: そ！　そ！　朝は元気よく！
English: That's the ticket! Attacking the day starts with energy in the morning!

Japanese: “早起きは３文の得”‥‥って、 ３文ぽっちかよ！　なーんて。
English: The early bird gets the worm. But then again, worms lack higher brain function.

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃひゃ！　あひゃ。 ホレ、マックス。牛乳１リットル。
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Here Max! I brought you a present! Have some milk.

Japanese: うぐおッ！ ‥‥サ、サンキュウ。
English: Oh my! Thank you!!

Japanese: どうだいニイちゃん、今日は。
English: So how are you today, Wright?

Japanese: ええ。どうやら、真犯人と 対決することになりそうですよ。
English: Well... I've got the feeling that today I'm going to face off against the real criminal.

Japanese: アクロ‥‥なのか？
English: You mean Acro?

Japanese: え！　そ、そうなの‥‥
English: Huh!? You think he did it...?

Japanese: ‥‥気をつけろ。
English: Be careful...

Japanese: アイツはいつも、ほそいロープの 上で、イノチを張ってきた。
English: He's used to putting his life on the line. Literally.

Japanese: ‥‥ドキョウは一流だぞ。
English: He's got guts to spare.

Japanese: わたってきたロープのほそさなら、 ぼくにも自信はありますよ。
English: If all I've got to worry about is how thin the tightrope is, I'm used to it already...

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく彼には、 ゆさぶりは効かないでしょう。
English: It just means that I won't be able to press him like I can other witnesses...

Japanese: え！　ど、どうするの！
English: What are you going to do then, Nick!?

Japanese: 今日は、いつもの 心理戦を捨てるよ。
English: I guess today we'll just have to do without our usual psychological warfare.

Japanese: ‥‥証拠品。彼を追いつめるには、 証拠のチカラしかない。
English: Today we rely on evidence. It's the only way we'll get past Acro and to the truth.

Japanese: うう‥‥キビシイなあ‥‥。
English: You're right... But it's gonna be tough...

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、たのむぜ。 ミリカに見せてやってくれ。
English: Anyways... I want you to make sure that Regina sees it all today. It's important.

Japanese: 殺人事件の“現実”を、 たっぷりとな。
English: Then she'll finally have to deal with the reality of what happened to her father.

Japanese: ‥‥ミリカちゃんに？
English: You want us to make sure Regina watches?

Japanese: ‥‥そのために、今日は あの子を連れてきたんだ。
English: Yes. That's why I brought her here to court today.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトだい？
English: What's that supposed to mean?

Japanese: 人は、死んだって星にはならない。
English: She needs to know that when people die, they don't just become stars.

Japanese: 時代おくれのピエロだって、 そんなの信じてない、ってコトさ。
English: I may be an old-fashioned clown, but I don't believe in people becoming stars.

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: December 30, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカの 法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session in the trial of Maximillion Galactica.

Japanese: 検察側、準備完了しているわ。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥では、狩魔検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Very well. Ms. von Karma, you may proceed with your case.

Japanese: ‥‥検察側は、意見を 変えざるをえなくなったわ。
English: The prosecution would like to revise it's previous theory of events.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What's the meaning of this?

Japanese: 新しい証人が現れた。 ‥‥彼もまた、目撃しているの。
English: We have discovered a new witness. Or shall I say, a new eyewitness.

Japanese: ‥‥マックス・ギャラクティカが、 犯行現場から飛び去るところを。
English: One that saw Maximillion Galactica fly off from the scene of the crime.

Japanese: 静粛に、静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥なんとなく、こうなる ような気がしていました。
English: I had a feeling something like this would come up...

Japanese: 検察側には、被告が飛び去った 方法を説明する用意がある。
English: Due to this revision, we are now prepared to explain how the defendant flew that night.

Japanese: ‥‥必要となれば、 のちほど提出するわ。
English: An explanation the prosecution will present if the need so arises...

Japanese: ウチの刑事に、 徹夜で模型を作らせたの。
English: In fact, my detective stayed up all night creating a mockup of the scene, on my orders.

Japanese: （かわいそうなイトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (Poor Gumshoe...)

Japanese: では、さっそく証人を 入廷させてください！
English: Very well... Please call your witness to the stand!

Japanese: （いよいよ、始まる！ ツナわたりの法廷が‥‥）
English: (Time to get to work! Or shall I say, time to walk the courtroom tightrope...)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。名前と、職業を。
English: Name and occupation.

Japanese: 木下　大作。みなさんからは “アクロ”と呼ばれています。
English: Ken Dingling, but everyone calls me Acro.

Japanese: タチミ・サーカスで、 アクロバットをやってました。
English: I'm employed as an acrobat at the Berry Big Circus.

Japanese: 証人。事件当夜は、どこに‥‥？
English: Where were you the night of the crime?

Japanese: はい。自分の部屋にいました。
English: I was in my room that night.

Japanese: 上面図でわかるとおり、証人の 部屋は、事件の現場付近にある。
English: If you look at the map, you will see the witness' room is near the crime scene.

Japanese: オレの部屋は３階にあります。 現場は、窓の下だったんです。
English: My room is on the third floor. The crime scene is below my window.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 事件当夜、証人は オドロクべきものを目撃した。
English: The night of the crime, the witness saw something quite shocking.

Japanese: ‥‥それを 証言してもらいましょう。
English: Would you tell us what you witnessed?

Japanese: わかりました。
English: OK...

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: 午後１０時をすぎていました。 オレ、ベッドで休んでいました。
English: It was just after 10:00 PM, and I was resting in my bed.

Japanese: そのとき、窓の下で“ドカッ”て 大きな音がしたんです。
English: Around that time, I heard a large "THUMP" noise from outside the window.

Japanese: しばらくすると‥‥窓のそとを、 人間が‥‥通りすぎていきました。
English: Then a few moments later, I saw someone... Flying... Right by my window.

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカ‥‥ うしろ姿だけど、そう見えました。
English: It was Max Galactica... I only saw him from behind, but that's who it looked like.

Japanese: そのときは、ユメだ、って 思ってたんですけどね‥‥。
English: To be honest, when I saw that, I thought I was dreaming...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥きのうのピエロの証言と ピッタリ一致しますね‥‥。
English: Hmm... This witness' testimony matches up exactly with that of the clown.

Japanese: こうなると、検察側としても さすがにムシできないわ。
English: If that's the case, there is very little the prosecution need add...

Japanese: ‥‥あとは、犯人が空に消えた、 その方法を説明するだけ。
English: All that's left is to explain how the defendant disappeared into the sky that night.

Japanese: その前に‥‥ 尋問をさせていただきます。
English: Before we get that far, I'd like to cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: ‥‥バカゆえに バカを演じるバカの悲しさ‥‥。
English: A foolish choice by a foolish fool who wishes to feel the foolish sadness of a sad fool.

Japanese: オトコは引きギワが カンジンよ、成歩堂　龍一。
English: A man must know the proper timing for things, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたのお友だち、 御剣　怜侍のようにね。
English: Just like your old friend, Mr. Miles Edgeworth did.

Japanese: 弁護人。 今の証言に、何か問題でも？
English: Mr. Wright. Do you have a problem with the witness' testimony?

Japanese: きのうの検察側のコトバを、 そのまま返します。
English: In the words of Ms. von Karma, may I quote yesterday's proceedings...

Japanese: 人は、空を飛びません！
English: "There's no way that actually happened!"

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 では、尋問をおねがいしましょう。
English: Very well... You may proceed with your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: 午後１０時をすぎていました。 オレ、ベッドで休んでいました。
English: It was just after 10:00 PM, and I was resting in my bed.

Japanese: そのとき、窓の下で“ドカッ”て 大きな音がしたんです。
English: Around that time, I heard a large "THUMP" noise from outside the window.

Japanese: しばらくすると‥‥窓のそとを、 人間が‥‥通りすぎていきました。
English: Then a few moments later, I saw someone... Flying... Right by my window.

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカ‥‥ うしろ姿だけど、そう見えました。
English: It was Max Galactica... I only saw him from behind, but that's who it looked like.

Japanese: そのときは、ユメだ、って 思ってたんですけどね‥‥。
English: To be honest, when I saw that, I thought I was dreaming...

Japanese: アクロさん‥‥やっぱり、 ウソついてるのかな‥‥？
English: Acro must be lying about things, huh?

Japanese: モチロンだよ。
English: Of course he is.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、勝負は、 そのウソを見破ってからだね。
English: Now the challenge will be to expose his lies in court.

Japanese: 負けないでね！
English: Well, put the pedal to the metal, Nick!

Japanese: ベッドで休んでいた、 ということは‥‥
English: You said that you were resting in bed.

Japanese: 部屋の電気は、 消していたわけですか？
English: One would thus assume that you turned the lights off in your room, correct?

Japanese: そうですね。
English: That's correct.

Japanese: ただ、宿舎のまわりには 常夜灯があるので、
English: But there are safety lights around the outside of the lodging house.

Japanese: 明るすぎて眠れない ぐらいなんですよ‥‥。
English: It's so bright, sometimes it can make it hard to sleep...

Japanese: あの。カーテンをしめれば いいのでは‥‥？
English: If that's the case, wouldn't it be a good idea to close some curtains?

Japanese: その発想はありませんでした。 さっそくカーテンを買います。
English: I never really thought of that. I guess that means I'm off to buy some curtains.

Japanese: ほっほっ。たまには私も 役に立つようですね。
English: Hah hah... Sometimes I do make myself useful in these chambers.

Japanese: はがむッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 証言をつづけて。
English: The witness will proceed with his testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥証人の部屋は３階に あるんですよね。
English: Your room is on the third floor, right?

Japanese: そうです。
English: Yes.

Japanese: しかも、証人はベッドで 寝ていた‥‥
English: And you said that you were resting in bed.

Japanese: まちがいありません。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: それでも音が 聞こえたんですか？
English: But you were still able to hear a sound from outside?

Japanese: ‥‥聞こえましたよ。
English: I was indeed.

Japanese: （‥‥アクロさんには、 ゆさぶりは通用しないだろう）
English: (Pressing Acro doesn't seem to get results...)

Japanese: （今の発言は‥‥どうだ？）
English: (Hmm... Maybe something was contradictory with what he just said...)

Japanese: どうしましたか？ ‥‥弁護人。
English: What's the matter, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: いえ‥‥なんでもありません。
English: N-N-Nothing, Your Honor.

Japanese: （決定的なムジュンが ないかぎり‥‥）
English: (Until I can find a clear contradiction...)

Japanese: （ヘタに動かないほうが いいだろう‥‥）
English: (I should tread lightly.)

Japanese: 今の証言には、大きな ムジュン点があります！
English: There is a huge contradiction with the testimony that was just given!

Japanese: ムジュンがあるのなら 証拠品に語らせなさい！
English: If there's a contradiction, then prove it with evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥そうですね。 見せていただきましょうか。
English: Hmm... She's right. Let's see some evidence.

Japanese: 今の発言にムジュンする 証拠とは、なんですか？
English: Do you have any evidence to support your claim of a contradiction?

Japanese: ほ、ほお。 ‥‥これはいったい‥‥
English: T-This... What in the world is this?

Japanese: あむぐッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、成歩堂　龍一。 わかっていないようね。
English: You just don't get it, do you Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥
English: W-What don't I get?

Japanese: すでに、ハッタリで 切り抜けられる状況じゃないの。
English: You're not going to be able to get through this one with your usual bluff routine!

Japanese: あなたの依頼人は、 すでに飛び立っているのよ！
English: You're client looks to be ready to take off once again!

Japanese: ‥‥有罪判決に向かって。
English: Straight towards a guilty verdict...

Japanese: は‥‥ハッタリだったんですか！
English: You were... B-Bluffing?

Japanese: 今度やったら ユルしませんぞお！
English: I will not forgive any more mockeries made of this court!

Japanese: （ま‥‥マイったな‥‥）
English: (W-Whoops!)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: Witness...

Japanese: もう一度、 証言してもらえるかしら。
English: Would you mind repeating your testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。わかりました。
English: Certainly...

Japanese: 本当にニンゲンだったのですか？
English: Are you sure it was a human being?

Japanese: マネキンとか、ビニールの人形 だったという可能性は‥‥！
English: It could've been a mannequin, or perhaps a large action figure...

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。マネキンは ともかく、ビニールってコトは‥‥
English: Hmm... Well, setting aside the possibility of a mannequin, an action figure is plausible...

Japanese: この弁護人に 気をつかうことはない。
English: You have no need to mince words with Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 真実を証言しなさい。 ‥‥証人が感じたままに。
English: Testify to the truth and only the truth. Just as if we were there with you that night...

Japanese: ニンゲンだったと思いますね。
English: I believe it was a human.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （くそ、かえって印象を 強くしちまったぞ‥‥）
English: (Damn... I just strengthened his testimony...)

Japanese: （今の発言は‥‥どうだ？）
English: (I wonder if Acro's statement jives with the facts...)

Japanese: どうしましたか？ ‥‥弁護人。
English: What's the matter, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: いえ‥‥なんでもありません。
English: N-N-Nothing, Your Honor.

Japanese: （決定的なムジュンが ないかぎり‥‥）
English: (Until I can find a clear contradiction...)

Japanese: （ヘタに動かないほうが いいだろう‥‥）
English: (I should tread lightly.)

Japanese: 部屋の電気は 消えていたんですよね？
English: The light in your room was turned off then, right?

Japanese: ええ。 ‥‥寝ていましたからね。
English: That's true. I was going to bed after all...

Japanese: それなのに、その人影が ハッキリ見えたんですか？
English: So with the lights off, you were still able to clearly see a human fly by your window?

Japanese: 常夜灯の明かりに 浮かび上がっていました。
English: The safety lights lit things up enough for me to see.

Japanese: たしかに、見えたのは リンカクだけです。
English: But honestly, there was only enough light for me to see the silhouette outside my window.

Japanese: うしろ姿でしたから、 白いバラも見えませんでした。
English: It was the person's back, so I couldn't see the white roses on the front.

Japanese: ‥‥他のシンボルは いかがでしたか？
English: Did you see any of the other symbols?

Japanese: シルクハットのかざりも、マントを つけた体つきもハッキリ見ました。
English: I clearly saw the silk hat, as well as the cloak wrapped around his body.

Japanese: オレが見たのは‥‥ マックスの人影でした。
English: I'm convinced that the person I saw was Max Galactica.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （ゆさぶればゆさぶるほど、 追いつめられていくな‥‥）
English: (The more I press him, the less results I seem to get...)

Japanese: （今の発言は‥‥どうだ？）
English: (But maybe there was something fishy with his latest bit of testimony?)

Japanese: どうしましたか？ ‥‥弁護人。
English: What's the matter, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: いえ‥‥なんでもありません。
English: N-N-Nothing, Your Honor.

Japanese: （決定的なムジュンが ないかぎり‥‥）
English: (Until I can find a clear contradiction...)

Japanese: （ヘタに動かないほうが いいだろう‥‥）
English: (I better not overdo it.)

Japanese: 今の証言には、大きな ムジュン点があります！
English: There is a huge contradiction with the testimony that was just given!

Japanese: ムジュンがあるのなら 証拠品に語らせなさい！
English: If there's a contradiction, then prove it with evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥そうですね。 見せていただきましょうか。
English: Hmm... She's right. Let's see some evidence.

Japanese: 今の発言にムジュンする 証拠とは、なんですか？
English: Do you have any evidence to support your claim of a contradiction?

Japanese: ほ、ほお。 ‥‥これはいったい‥‥
English: T-This... What in the world is this?

Japanese: あむぐッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、成歩堂　龍一。 わかっていないようね。
English: You just don't get it, do you Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥
English: W-What don't I get?

Japanese: すでに、ハッタリで 切り抜けられる状況じゃないの。
English: You're not going to be able to get through this one with your usual bluff routine!

Japanese: あなたの依頼人は、 すでに飛び立っているのよ！
English: You're client looks to be ready to take off once again!

Japanese: ‥‥有罪判決に向かって。
English: Straight towards a guilty verdict...

Japanese: は‥‥ハッタリだったんですか！
English: You were... B-Bluffing?

Japanese: 今度やったら ユルしませんぞお！
English: I will not forgive any more mockeries made of this court!

Japanese: （ま‥‥マイったな‥‥）
English: (W-Whoops!)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: Witness...

Japanese: もう一度、 証言してもらえるかしら。
English: Would you mind repeating your testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。わかりました。
English: With pleasure...

Japanese: いつから“ユメじゃない”と 思い始めたんですか？
English: When did you start thinking that what you saw "wasn't a dream"?

Japanese: そんなコトは重要では‥‥
English: That's not important...

Japanese: どこかの検事がキオクの操作を したかもしれません！
English: I believe that the prosecution has done a bit of maintenance on the witness' memory!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥念のため、 証人に聞いておきましょうか‥‥
English: Hmm... Just to make sure, let's ask the witness.

Japanese: はみぎッ！
English: OWWW!!!

Japanese: ‥‥異議あり。
English: Objection...

Japanese: み‥‥認めます。
English: S-Sustained...

Japanese: （認めるなよ！）
English: (Don't sustain that!!)

Japanese: あなたも、見たわけですね。 トミーさんと同じモノを‥‥
English: You claim to have seen the exact same thing Moe saw that night. Do you stand by that?

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: シルクハットです。
English: The silk hat.

Japanese: それがどうかしましたか？
English: What about the silk hat? I saw it on Max's head as he flew by my window.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、窓から下を のぞいてみるべきでしたね。
English: Well... You should have tried looking down out of your window that night.

Japanese: オレ、今はこんなカラダ ですからね。
English: That would have been quite difficult considering the state that I'm in.

Japanese: 窓のそとを見るのも、 けっこう大変なんですよ。
English: Just looking outside of the window was a tough enough challenge for me.

Japanese: これは、現場にころがっていた シルクハットです。
English: That's a shame, because you would have noticed the silk hat found on the scene.

Japanese: それは‥‥ 団長のもの、では‥‥？
English: That... That's the Ringmaster's hat... Right?

Japanese: どう見ても、 マックスのシルクハットです。
English: Afraid not. No matter how you look at it, this is Max's silk hat.

Japanese: これはいったい、 どういうことですか！
English: Where are you going with this, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: マックスさんの シルクハットが‥‥２個！
English: Are you saying that Max has two silk hats!?

Japanese: シルクハットは特注で、 この世に１つしかありません。
English: No. This is a handmade, one-of-a-kind model made only for Maximillion Galactica.

Japanese: ということは‥‥アクロさん。
English: Which means, Acro...

Japanese: あなたはどこかで、 ウソをついているのです！
English: That you've been fibbing on the stand!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥いつものコトながら、 考えなしにしゃべるオトコね。
English: Like always, someone has to open their mouth before thinking...

Japanese: だ、だいじょうぶ？ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Are you OK, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥言っちゃったからには、 攻めるしかないね。
English: Well, I opened my big mouth, and now I have to back it up...

Japanese: 弁護人。うかがいましょうか。
English: How about it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: この証人がウソをつく理由は なんですか‥‥？
English: What would cause this witness to commit perjury in this court today?

Japanese: おそらくアクロさんは、 マックスにウラミがあるのです！
English: Perhaps it is because Acro has a grudge against the defendant!

Japanese: だから、彼に罪を 着せようとして‥‥
English: Which is why he is trying to frame Max...

Japanese: はぐあッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥なな、なんで私なんですか！
English: Why me!? What'd I do!?

Japanese: ‥‥ムチの通り道に ジャマなアタマを置かないで。
English: Never get your fat head in the way of a whip bent on finding justice!

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一。‥‥あなたの スイリなど聞きたくもない。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright... I have no interest in your silly little theories!

Japanese: “被告人をウラんでいた” ‥‥そんな証拠はあるの？
English: He has a grudge against the defendant, huh? You wouldn't happen to have proof of that?

Japanese: う。そ、それは‥‥
English: Well... Umm... That's the thing...

Japanese: 弁護人！　あなたのおかげで 私はたたかれたんですぞ！
English: Mr. Wright! Thanks to you, I got my head lashed by Ms. von Karma's whip!

Japanese: ちゃんと、証明できる コタエを考えてください！
English: I don't like getting hit for nothing, so how about you answer with an explanation!

Japanese: アクロさんが見たものは、 なんとユメだったのです！
English: It's obvious that what Acro saw, he didn't actually see at all, because it was a dream!

Japanese: ツゴウの悪いことはみんな、 ユメでカタづけるつもり？
English: So you plan on writing off all the inconvenient facts as nothing but dreams?

Japanese: ユメかどうか、ホッペを つねってたしかめるがいいわ！
English: Maybe you should just pinch your cheeks and see if you are actually awake.

Japanese: いてッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: （ホッペをつねる前に、 すでにムチがイタい‥‥）
English: (I think your whip proved this isn't a dream just fine...)

Japanese: 弁護人！　ちゃんと証明できる コタエを考えてください！
English: Mr. Wright! How about you provide an answer that actually explains things!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: この際、弁護側は アクロ氏を告発します！
English: On this occasion, the defense accuses Acro himself!
"On this occasion"...?

Japanese: こ‥‥“告発”ですって！ いったい、なんの‥‥
English: "A-A-Accuses Acro"!? What in the world are you accusing him of?

Japanese: モチロン、立見　七百人 殺害の真犯人として、です！
English: Obviously we accuse him of the murder of Mr. Russell Berry!

Japanese: ほ‥‥ホンキですか 成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright, are you serious!?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Deadly serious, Your Honor.

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥ サーカスに出入りするうち、
English: Ha ha ha... I think your trips to the circus have served you well...

Japanese: 観客のココロをつかむ コツをおぼえたみたいね‥‥
English: You seem to remember how to try and grab at an audience's hearts and minds.

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥おどろくにはあたらないわ。
English: Your Honor. Don't allow yourself to be swayed by theatrics.

Japanese: 傍聴人の気を引こうとする、 むなしいハッタリよ。
English: Trying to wow the crowd with smoke and mirrors is the oldest bluff in the book.

Japanese: そ‥‥ そうなのですか‥‥？
English: R-Really...?

Japanese: その証拠に、 証人をごらんなさい。
English: If you don't believe me, just look at the witness.

Japanese: ‥‥ニクらしいほど 落ちついてるわ。
English: He's calm enough for it to almost be scary...

Japanese: （うう‥‥マユひとつ、 動かさないぞ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... He is staying rather calm and collected...)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥弁護人の告発に対して、 何か言いたいことは‥‥？
English: Mr. Dingling, do you have any response to the defense's accusation?

Japanese: ‥‥何も言う必要はないでしょう？
English: I don't really need to say a thing, do I?

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: みなさん。オレを見てください。
English: Everyone, take a good look at me.

Japanese: ひとりじゃ、宿舎を出ることは おろか、立つこともできない。
English: I can't even stand up by myself, let alone leave the lodging house.

Japanese: ‥‥た‥‥たしかに。
English: T-That's true...

Japanese: 弁護士さんが、マックスを 助けたいのはリカイできます。
English: I understand that Mr. Wright is just trying to help his client.

Japanese: でも‥‥よりによって、 このオレを犯人だなんて‥‥
English: But to do this by accusing me of a murder of all things...

Japanese: そのとおり！ ヒジョーシキにもほどがあるわ！
English: See! Even a silver of common sense makes it clear the accusation is ludicrous!

Japanese: そうだ！　ヒドいぞ！
English: She's right! Way to pick on the disabled you heartless, cruel man!

Japanese: ケガ人に、なんてコト言うんだ！
English: Phoenix is a poopyhead!!

Japanese: ‥‥わかった？　成歩堂　龍一。
English: See that, Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: 傍聴人を味方につけるなら、 こういうふうにやるものよ。
English: If you're trying to drum up support from the peanut gallery, that's how you do it.

Japanese: ‥‥うう。
English: Uhh...

Japanese: おあそびはここまでよ。
English: I think that's enough of this little game.

Japanese: 証人が立てないことについては、 医師の証明書があるわ！
English: I've got a doctor's note to confirm that Acro is unable to stand under his own power.

Japanese: それとも弁護人は、 こう主張するつもり？
English: Maybe the defense is planning on making a claim to counter this as well?

Japanese: “アクロ氏には共犯者がいた” ‥‥とでも！
English: I can hear the defense now... "Acro had an accomplice!!"

Japanese: どうなんですか弁護人！ この犯行には、共犯者が‥‥？
English: What do you say about this, Mr. Wright? Did Acro have an accomplice?

Japanese: アクロさんに犯行は不可能。
English: It's impossible for Acro to be the murderer in this case.

Japanese: そうなれば当然、 共犯者の存在が考えられます！
English: If that's the case, then it's natural for one to consider possible accomplices!

Japanese: ふ、ふむう‥‥な、なるほど‥‥
English: Hmm... You don't say...

Japanese: （あぶないあぶない。 ‥‥なんとか、ピンチを‥‥）
English: (Not good... Not good... This isn't going to end well.)

Japanese: 切りぬけた、とでも 考えているのかしら？
English: Don't tell me you think you're gonna get off easy.

Japanese: な、ナニっ！
English: W-WHAT!?

Japanese: ‥‥ホッとするあまり、 聞き逃したみたいね‥‥。
English: You must not have heard that sound just now... Maybe it wasn't clear enough...

Japanese: ワナの口が開く音、を。
English: The sound of you falling into your own trap can be hard to notice sometimes!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: あなたは、アクロ氏ばかりでなく、 さらに告発をしようとしている。
English: It seems as if you won't be stopping at Acro, but will be making other accusations.

Japanese: その人物が共犯者でなかったら ‥‥タイヘンなコトになりますぞ！
English: If Acro did not have an accomplice, things are not looking good for you!

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。
English: I understand, Your Honor. *gulp*

Japanese: では、うかがいましょう。 “共犯者”とは、だれですか？
English: Very well... Let's continue. Who, perchance, would happen to be Acro's accomplice?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、 そうだったのですか‥‥！
English: You... You don't say!

Japanese: （あぶないあぶない。 ‥‥なんとか、ピンチを‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... This is suddenly looking very very bad...)

Japanese: 切りぬけた、とでも 考えているのかしら？
English: Don't tell me you think you're gonna get off easy.

Japanese: ななな、ナニっ！
English: W-W-W-WHAT!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやらまた、 聞き逃したみたいね‥‥。
English: Oh... Did you hear that?

Japanese: “聞き逃した”‥‥？
English: Hear what?

Japanese: ワナの口が閉じる音、よ。
English: The sound of you getting caught in your own trap!

Japanese: ‥‥共犯者、おおいにケッコウ。 それなら、コンキョを聞かせて。
English: What a splendid accomplice. I'm sure this accusation is built on a solid foundation.

Japanese: コンキョ‥‥。
English: Foundation...

Japanese: そうですね。 ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Yes, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: その人物が共犯者であるという 証拠を提示してください。
English: Do you have any proof that this person you've accused was indeed an accomplice?

Japanese: 弁護人‥‥。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: この証拠品には、 みじんの説得力も感じられません。
English: This piece of evidence is lacking in persuasive power.

Japanese: 今度こそ、聞き逃すことはないわ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: You didn't miss that sound, did you Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: ペナルティを受ける、 この音を！
English: You know... The sound of you receiving a penalty!!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥！）
English: (Arrrrghhhh!)

Japanese: ダメだよなるほどくん。 もっとよく、考えないと‥‥
English: What are you doing, Nick!?

Japanese: さあ！　共犯者の名前を 聞かせてもらおうかしら！
English: Now then, this must be when we get to hear the name of the mystery accomplice!

Japanese: ‥‥ムダだ、狩魔検事。
English: Not this time, Von Karma.

Japanese: な‥‥ナニッ！
English: Wh... WHAT!?

Japanese: その手にはもう、乗らない。
English: You're not going to sucker me into this one.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか、 成歩堂くん！
English: What are you blabbering about, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 共犯者などいない！
English: There was no accomplice!

Japanese: これは、アクロさん１人が計画し、 そして実行した事件なのです！
English: Acro planned and committed this murder all by himself!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-Order! Order!

Japanese: こ、これはいったい‥‥、 その、なんというか‥‥
English: What the... What are you getting at!?

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Way to keep them on their toes, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥ここからは、一気に 立証しなければならない。
English: Now I'm going to have to prove how it all fits together...

Japanese: “アクロさんがどうやって、 立見団長を殺害したか？”‥‥
English: I have to show how Acro murdered Russell Berry.

Japanese: ‥‥できるの？ そんなコト‥‥！
English: Can you do it Nick? Can you really do that!?

Japanese: 立ち止まるワケにはいかない。 攻撃をやめたら‥‥負ける！
English: I know what I can't do. I can't stop now... If I stop attacking, I'm doomed!

Japanese: ようし‥‥ いっちょ、やるよ！
English: Alright! Then let's do it!!

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: この証人が犯人なら、さっきの 目撃証言はウソ、というワケね？
English: If this witness is the killer, then his eyewitness account is all lies, right?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥では、弁護人。 まず、教えてください。
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright, I'd like you to clear something up for me...

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、 アクロさんは、どこにいたのか？
English: When the crime was committed, exactly where was Mr. Dingling?

Japanese: ここ以外、あり得ません！
English: There is no way he was anywhere but exactly right here!

Japanese: 広場‥‥殺人現場ですか？
English: In the plaza? He was at the scene of the crime itself?

Japanese: たしかに、被害者に 会わなければ、犯行は‥‥
English: You're right though... The murderer would have had to have met the victim...

Japanese: このあたり‥‥、 だと思われます！
English: He was right here!

Japanese: ほほお‥‥どうも事件とは むすびつきませんね‥‥。
English: What!? That location is completely unrelated to the crime...

Japanese: まあたしかに、自分の部屋に いたのでは、犯行はムリ‥‥
English: But I suppose if you said he was in his room, he wouldn't have been able to commit...

Japanese: ‥‥いてッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ここ、コラコラ！　狩魔検事！
English: Ms. von Karma! Stop it!!

Japanese: ‥‥無言でぶたないように！
English: You will be quiet and refrain from striking people!

Japanese: 証人は、自分の力では 宿舎から出られなかったのよ！
English: The witness could not have possibly left the lodging house on his own!

Japanese: しかもさっき、あなたは ハッキリこう言った！
English: Not to mention what Mr. Phoenix Wright just said!

Japanese: 『この事件に、共犯者はいない』
English: "There was no accomplice!" remember?

Japanese: そ、そうでした。 もう一度、おねがいします！
English: Yeah... Um... You're right. You think I could get another chance?

Japanese: ちょっと待ちなさい。
English: Wait just one minute.

Japanese: ‥‥私からのムチも、 忘れないように。
English: Don't forget about my proverbial whip as well.

Japanese: （ぐぐぐぐぐぐ‥‥）
English: (Arrrrgh!)

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、ここです。
English: He was obviously here the entire time.

Japanese: そこは‥‥ あ、アクロさんの部屋‥‥？
English: That's... Acro's room?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなことです。
English: Pretty simple, eh?

Japanese: アクロさんは、ひとりでは 宿舎から出られなかった。
English: Acro wasn't able to leave the lodging house by himself.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば、コタエは１つ。
English: There's only one answer to that...

Japanese: アクロさんは部屋から出ることなく 立見団長を殺害したのです！
English: Acro didn't leave his room to kill the Ringmaster!

Japanese: ナニをバカなッ！
English: What!? Are you nuts!?

Japanese: 証人！　いかがですかッ！
English: What say you, Mr. Dingling!?

Japanese: おもしろいですね。
English: It's an interesting theory.

Japanese: あの。それだけですか。
English: Umm... That's it?

Japanese: だって‥‥そんなこと、 ムリですからね。
English: Considering that what you proposed is impossible, yes, that's it.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。成歩堂くん。
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 証人が言うとおり‥‥ あなたの主張にはムリがあります。
English: As the witness has stated... Your assertion is impossible.

Japanese: 車イスに座ったまま、現場にも 行かずに、犯行をなしとげた‥‥。
English: As he is in a wheelchair, there is no way he could go to the scene or be the killer.

Japanese: ええ‥‥そうなります。
English: Hmm... You've got a point.

Japanese: 忘れているようね‥‥ 成歩堂　龍一。
English: It seems you've forgotten once again, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 現場では、被告のすがたが ハッキリ目撃されているのよ。
English: The defendant was clearly spotted at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: そ、そうだよ！
English: That's true!

Japanese: トミーさんも、言ってたじゃない！ マックスが‥‥
English: Moe said that he saw Max, didn't he?

Japanese: ‥‥でも、真宵ちゃん。 人間は空を飛ばないよ。
English: But Maya, it's still impossible for humans to fly.

Japanese: ‥‥１つ、聞いていいですか？
English: Do you mind if I ask a a question, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: なんでしょう。
English: What is it?

Japanese: “オレが、団長を‥‥” それはわかりました。
English: I understand some of your logic...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。 いったい、どうやって？
English: However, how do you think that I killed him?

Japanese: 部屋から出られず、もちろん マックスの衣装も着られない‥‥
English: If I can't leave my room, I obviously couldn't wear Max's costume...

Japanese: （そう‥‥“どうやって？”）
English: (Hmm... How did he do it?)

Japanese: （それが、次のポイントだ‥‥）
English: (That's the next course of this legal buffet...)

Japanese: だいじょうぶ？ ここでまちがったら‥‥
English: Be careful, Nick. If you mess up here...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ よく考えなければ！）
English: (She's right. I can't mess up here. I've gotta give this one some serious thought!)

Japanese: （アクロさんは、自分の部屋に いながらにして‥‥）
English: (I'm sure that Acro killed the Ringmaster...)

Japanese: （‥‥立見団長を殺害した！ それはマチガイない‥‥）
English: (And he killed the Ringmaster while he was in his room... No doubt about it!)

Japanese: では、アクロ氏の犯行の方法を 示してください！
English: Time to enlighten us as to how Mr. Dingling committed the crime, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: さあ！　犯行の方法を 聞かせてもらおうかしら！
English: Ahh... I think we will finally be graced with an explanation!

Japanese: ‥‥ムダだ、狩魔検事。
English: Don't push it, Von Karma!

Japanese: な‥‥ナニッ！
English: Wha... WHAT!?

Japanese: その手にはもう、乗らない。
English: You aren't gonna suck me in this time.

Japanese: ‥‥だって、提示できる証拠 なんて、ありませんからね！
English: I'm smart enough to know when I don't have the cards to play and my hand sucks right now.

Japanese: ‥‥は、はあ。 ということは‥‥？
English: Uhh... What do you mean, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 今までの弁護人のスイリは、 すべて無意味だ、というコトね。
English: He means that everything he's said up until now has all been nonsense.

Japanese: え。
English: Uh.

Japanese: トーゼンでしょ？ 犯行方法が立証できないのなら。
English: It's true, right? If you can't establish proof of your murder theory, then it's nonsense!

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Uhhh...

Japanese: カッコつかないカッコつけかた だったわね‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: He's trying to fool us by playing some misdirection game.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、ここじゃ 通用しないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like this isn't going to pass muster...)

Japanese: ダメだよなるほどくん！ とりあえず出そうよ！　証拠。
English: Nick! You've gotta show them some evidence!

Japanese: 出す、って言ってもなあ‥‥
English: Easier said than done...

Japanese: さあ！　どうしますか ‥‥弁護人！
English: So what will it be, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 では、証拠品をお見せします！
English: I'm going to present some evidence!

Japanese: アクロ氏は、何を使って 被害者を殺害したのですか？
English: So what did Mr. Dingling use to commit the crime of murder against Russell Berry?

Japanese: ‥‥何か言われる前に、 やりなおしたほうがいいよ。
English: I think you should try again, especially before someone gets the chance to say something.

Japanese: そうみたいだね。
English: Yeah, you're probably right.

Japanese: えー、そういったワケで。 この証拠品を‥‥
English: Umm... So as I was saying... This piece of evidence--

Japanese: コラコラ、待ちなさい！ その前に、ペナルティを与えます！
English: Stop! Stop! Wait, I say! Before you start over, you must be penalized!

Japanese: （チッ、おそかったか‥‥）
English: (Doh! He caught me...)

Japanese: ‥‥イイカゲンにしないと、 私にも考えがありますぞ！
English: And if you don't put an end to this funny business, I've got a few more ideas!

Japanese: なんですか、それ。 写真、ですか‥‥？
English: What's that? A picture?

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、問題は これに写っているシロモノです。
English: It is indeed. The problem is with the item that's shown in the picture.

Japanese: 胸像‥‥ですか。
English: The bust?

Japanese: 等身大のね。 ‥‥かなりの重量があります。
English: It's quite a large bust. And because it is life sized, it is also very very heavy.
Heavy...?

Japanese: まちがいなく死ぬでしょうね。
English: Heavy enough to guarantee a certain death.

Japanese: ‥‥３階の窓から、 こんなのが落ちてきたら。
English: Especially if it was dropped from a third story window.

Japanese: ‥‥ああああッ！
English: AHHH!!

Japanese: そう！　アクロさんは、 コイツで団長を殺害したんです！
English: See! This is how Acro was able to kill the Ringmaster!

Japanese: マックスの胸像と、 そして‥‥重力で！
English: With the force of gravity and Maximillion Galactica's ample bust!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 胸像を‥‥ “落とした”ですってェ！
English: So you're saying the bust fell onto the Ringmaster!?

Japanese: ‥‥シンプルな犯行です。
English: A rather simple crime...

Japanese: これならば、車イスに 座っていても‥‥
English: Even if you were stuck in a wheelchair, it would be incredibly easy to commit...

Japanese: そんな大きなものを持って、 車イスの操作など不可能よ！
English: How could you possibly wheel a wheelchair with something so heavy!? It's impossible!

Japanese: アクロさんは アクロバットの芸人です！
English: Well, Acro is an acrobat!

Japanese: これぐらいの像をはこぶのは、 たやすいでしょう！
English: He should have more than enough upper body strength to carry something like the bust!

Japanese: 証人！　アクロさん！ ‥‥い、いかがですか‥‥？
English: Mr. Dingling, how do you respond to these charges!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: アクロさんが‥‥ コトバに詰まったよ！
English: Acro's at a loss for words!

Japanese: （そりゃそうだろう‥‥ ズバリ、核心だからな！）
English: (He should be... He knows that I'm getting close to the truth!)

Japanese: さあ、アクロさん！ もう、今度こそ逃げられ
English: Well well well, Acro. You can't run away from things this time...

Japanese: いててッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥そうは行かないわ、 成歩堂　龍一‥‥！
English: I'd watch what I say, if I were you, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: 裁判長！　証人の身体能力は、 重要なポイントよ。
English: Your Honor! The physical health of the witness is material to this case!

Japanese: 本人の口から、セイカクに 証言させることを要求するわ。
English: I demand that we get proper testimony from the witness himself!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ 証言、ですか‥‥。
English: Hmm... Testimony you say...

Japanese: （狩魔のヤツ‥‥）
English: (Von Karma...)

Japanese: （“証言”というカタチをとって、 時間をかせぐつもりだな！）
English: (She's just using this "testimony" as a rouse to stall for time!)

Japanese: その必要はありません！
English: There is absolutely no need for such testimony!

Japanese: 弁護側が犯行方法を提示した！ 検察側には反証するケンリがある！
English: The defense has its version of the murder. The prosecution has the right to respond!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側の異議を却下します。
English: The defense's objection is overruled...

Japanese: （しまった‥‥！）
English: (Why can't he see things my way once in a while!)

Japanese: 証人。申しわけありませんが、 身体状況を証言してください。
English: Mr. Dingling, I'm sorry, but we need you to testify about your physical condition.

Japanese: ギモンがある場合は、医師の 出廷を求めることになります。
English: If you have any doubts about your ability to testify, we can request expert testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。アセることはない。 ゆっくり証言するといいわ。
English: The witness will have no problems; however, let's all be respectful towards him.

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you...

Japanese: （くそッ！　追いつめる チャンスだったのに‥‥）
English: (Argh! That woman will sink to any low to win a case!)

Japanese: ～アクロの身体能力～
English: -- Acro's Physical State --

Japanese: たしかに、胸像ぐらいでしたら、 持ち上げられると思いますよ。
English: I suppose I could have lifted something the size of that bust.

Japanese: オレ、上半身はきたえてあるし、 ケガもしてませんからね。
English: I have a strong upper body from working as an acrobat, and only my legs were injured.

Japanese: ただ‥‥胸像を持って、窓から 身を乗り出すのはムリですね。
English: However, lifting the bust and looking out of the window would've been impossible.

Japanese: オレの下半身は、今はチカラを 入れることができないんですよ。
English: There's no way I could have exerted that kind of force on my lower body.

Japanese: 団長のアタマの位置を知ることは 不可能だったわけです。
English: That makes it impossible for me to have known the location of the Ringmaster's head.

Japanese: オレに胸像を落とすのはムリだ、 って、わかってもらえるでしょう？
English: Thus it would be unrealistic for me to drop the bust on him. Don't you think?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。証言に 不審なところはありませんね。
English: Hmm... I have no doubts in regards to this witness' testimony.

Japanese: 重い胸像を持ったまま、 窓から身を乗り出すのは不可能。
English: It was impossible for him to lift the bust and carry it over to the window.

Japanese: その上で、被害者のアタマを 狙うなんて、キケンすぎるわ。
English: Not to mention that he could not have known the location of the Ringmaster's head.

Japanese: シッパイしたら、 身のハメツなのだから‥‥。
English: A single false step would have lead to even more severe injuries.

Japanese: そのとおりですな。 ‥‥いかがですか、成歩堂くん。
English: That's what I was thinking. What is your opinion on the matter, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: とりあえず、尋問を。
English: I'd still like to proceed with my cross-examination.

Japanese: 時間かせぎ、ってトコロね。 ‥‥みっともないオトコ。
English: He's simply stalling. It's shameful, really.

Japanese: （くそぉ‥‥ 言われたほうが負け、か‥‥）
English: (Grr... I can't let her get to me... I've got to focus...)

Japanese: ～アクロの身体能力～
English: -- Acro's Physical State --

Japanese: たしかに、胸像ぐらいでしたら、 持ち上げられると思いますよ。
English: I suppose I could have lifted something the size of that bust.

Japanese: オレ、上半身はきたえてあるし、 ケガもしてませんからね。
English: I have a strong upper body from working as an acrobat, and only my legs were injured.

Japanese: ただ‥‥胸像を持って、窓から 身を乗り出すのはムリですね。
English: However, lifting the bust and looking out of the window would've been impossible.

Japanese: オレの下半身は、今はチカラを 入れることができないんですよ。
English: There's no way I could have exerted that kind of force on my lower body.

Japanese: 団長のアタマの位置を知ることは 不可能だったわけです。
English: That makes it impossible for me to have known the location of the Ringmaster's head.

Japanese: オレに胸像を落とすのはムリだ、 って、わかってもらえるでしょう？
English: Thus it would be unrealistic for me to drop the bust on him. Don't you think?

Japanese: （アクロさんは、胸像を落とす チカラはあった‥‥）
English: (Acro is strong enough to lift up the bust...)

Japanese: モンダイは、団長さんのアタマを 狙えなかった、ってコトだね。
English: The main problem is how he could have aimed for the Ringmaster's head.

Japanese: アタマを狙うために、何か 道具でも使ったのかなあ‥‥？
English: Hmm... I wonder if he used some kind of tool to aim for the Ringmaster...?

Japanese: それだッ、なるほどくん！ つきつけてやろうよ！
English: That's the ticket, Nick! Show them what you've got!

Japanese: いやいや。 “あったとすれば”だよ。
English: I have to be careful. I have to find something that fits perfectly with the case.

Japanese: でも、なかったら できなかったんでしょ？
English: Hmm... I don't remember us finding any sort of tool...

Japanese: じゃあさ。きっと、 あったんだよ‥‥何か。
English: But maybe we overlooked something...

Japanese: あの胸像、持ったことが あるんですか‥‥？
English: Have you ever lifted up the bust before...?

Japanese: ‥‥いや、それはまあ、 ありませんけど。
English: No, I've never actually lifted it up with my own two hands...

Japanese: さすがに、ルーサーには 負けられませんからね。
English: But I should get to it, don't you think? I can't let Money outdo me on this.

Japanese: ‥‥ルーサー‥‥？
English: Money...?

Japanese: （あのサル、マックスの胸像を 持ち上げたことがあるのか？）
English: (That crazy monkey has lifted Max's bust before?)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 証人のケガは、下半身だけ、 と考えていいわけですか。
English: Please continue with your testimony, Mr. Dingling.

Japanese: ‥‥最近は、トレーニングは？
English: So what have you been doing to keep in shape?

Japanese: それはさすがに、ね‥‥。 やめましたよ。
English: Well, honestly, I've given up on training.

Japanese: もう、ブランコやロープの上に もどるつもりはありませんから。
English: I don't have any plans to return to the trapeze or the tightrope.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: You don't say...

Japanese: でも、シツレイですが、あなたには 負けませんよ、成歩堂さん。
English: But, no offense, I'm not worried about losing to you in a race or anything.

Japanese: 私もあなたには負けないわ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Neither am I, Mr. Phoenix Wright!!

Japanese: あたしだって負けないからね！ なるほどくん！
English: I wouldn't lose either, slow poke! ...I mean Nick!

Japanese: （な、なんかイジめられてる 気がするぞ‥‥）
English: (Whoa whoa whoa! How did the discussion turn to me all of a sudden!?)

Japanese: とにかくオレ、チカラは だれにも負けませんけど‥‥、
English: I suppose you could say that I'm stronger than the average bear...

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: And why is that?

Japanese: だって、そんなことしたら、 オレのほうが落っこちますよ。
English: Because if I were to do that, I'd end up falling out the window myself.

Japanese: まだ、下半身にはカンカクが もどってませんからね。
English: I still haven't gotten much feeling back in my legs yet.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥足を動かすことも できないわけですか？
English: Hmm... So you couldn't have thrown that bust out of the window...

Japanese: なおるまでには、 どれぐらい‥‥？
English: How long do you think your recovery will take?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。なにしろ、 神経をやられてますからね。
English: Hmm... You have to remember that the nerves were severed.

Japanese: 今、ハードなリハビリテーションを してますけど、
English: I'm currently undergoing some extremely intensive rehabilitation,

Japanese: まだまだ‥‥という 感じですね。
English: but it's still going to take a while before I'm back to 100%.

Japanese: ‥‥重要なポイントは１つ。
English: Let me remind you of another very important point.

Japanese: 胸像を持ったまま、証人が 窓の下を見ることはできなかった。
English: If the witness was carrying the bust, he would not be able to see out below the window.

Japanese: どうして“不可能” なんですか？
English: Why do you say it would be impossible?

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか？
English: Allow me to explain.

Japanese: 胸像を持ったまま、窓の下を 見ることができなかったんですよ？
English: You accept that if I was carrying the bust, I couldn't see out below the window?

Japanese: 団長のアタマがどこにあるか、 わかるはずがないでしょう。
English: Thus, there is no way that I would know the location of the Ringmaster's head.

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあ‥‥ そういうコトになりますね。
English: Well... I suppose you've got a point.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん？
English: Hey, Nick?
Huh?

Japanese: じゃあさ。発想を逆転させれば ‥‥こういうコト？
English: What if you turned things around? Maybe if you think of it sorta like this...

Japanese: “被害者のアタマの位置さえ わかれば、胸像を落とせた”
English: "If he knew the location of the Ringmaster's head, then he could drop the bust."

Japanese: そういうコトに‥‥なるね。
English: That does make sense...

Japanese: （“団長のアタマの位置を、 窓から下を見ないで知る”‥‥）
English: (If only I could prove somehow...)

Japanese: （そんな方法を示せれば いいんだな‥‥）
English: (That Acro knew the location of the Ringmaster's head without looking down...)

Japanese: もう、じゅうぶんだと 思いますけど。
English: I think I've already explained things sufficiently.

Japanese: 落とすだけなら、 あなたにもできたはずです！
English: If all you had to do was drop it, then it wouldn't have been a problem at all!

Japanese: 落とすだけなら‥‥ そう。できたでしょうね。
English: If all I had to do was drop it... You're right. I could've done that;

Japanese: ‥‥でも、団長のアタマに 当てるのは、ゼッタイにムリです。
English: however, there is no way that I could score a direct hit on the Ringmaster's head.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥それではイミが ありませんねえ‥‥。
English: Hmm... So that kind of makes your theory a bit pointless, doesn't it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥アクロさん。
English: Acro...

Japanese: あなたは、窓から身を乗り出す 必要はなかったんです。
English: You didn't really need to lean out of the window, did you?

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことでしょうか？
English: What are you driving at, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: あなたには、前もって わかっていたんです。
English: You already knew ahead of time where the Ringmaster's head was going to be.

Japanese: 団長のアタマが、どこにあるのか。 ‥‥それも、きわめて正確にね。
English: Quite precisely I may add.

Japanese: ジラそうとしたってムダよ！ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: Your silly hinting at things is pointless! Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: もったいぶらずに、ホラ！ さっさと証拠を見せなさいッ！
English: Enough stalling! How about you show us some evidence!

Japanese: （‥‥思いっきり ジラされてるくせに）
English: (But... But I did such a good job hinting...)

Japanese: そうです！ 早く説明を！　弁護人！
English: Yes yes yes! Hurry up and explain things, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥これを見ていただきましょう。
English: Maybe you should take a look at this...

Japanese: ‥‥ポイントは、“木箱”です。
English: The key point here is the "wooden box".

Japanese: 木箱‥‥というと、被害者が 突っぷしている‥‥？
English: The same wooden box that the victim was found hunched over...?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: The same.

Japanese: ‥‥この木箱は、いったい だれが置いたんでしょう？
English: The question is, who placed the wooden box here?
Who...?

Japanese: ベンさんたちが団長を見たとき、 彼はそんなものは持ってなかった。
English: When Ben and company saw the Ringmaster, they didn't see him holding the box.

Japanese: とすれば‥‥
English: Which means...

Japanese: この木箱は、現場に 置かれていたことになります。
English: That this wooden box was already placed at the scene crime.

Japanese: と‥‥当然、 そうなりますね‥‥
English: I have to admit that your theory makes a lot of sense...

Japanese: 胸像が落下してくる瞬間‥‥、
English: The moment that the bust came falling down...

Japanese: 団長は、この木箱を 持ち上げようとしていたのです。
English: Was exactly the same moment that the Ringmaster lifted up this wooden box.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなれば、 もう答えはアキラカでしょう！
English: Which means that the answer to all these questions is now crystal clear!

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか！
English: You... You mean!

Japanese: ‥‥木箱の置かれていたポイントに 胸像を落とせば‥‥
English: If the bust were to fall upon the point marked out by the wooden box...

Japanese: まちがいなく、被害者の アタマにヒットするのです！
English: There would be no way that it could miss the head of the victim!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなあッ！
English: R-Really...!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥ま、まさか‥‥そんなことが！
English: Order! Order! Order! This is unbelievable!!

Japanese: （‥‥よし！　なんとか スイリがつながったぞ‥‥）
English: (Finally! Some of these loose ends are starting to tie themselves up!)

Japanese: （このまま突き進むんだ！ ‥‥とにかく、前へ‥‥）
English: (Now I just gotta keep going! And there's only one way to go from here... Forward.)

Japanese: で‥‥では、木箱を現場に 置いたのは‥‥？
English: So the next question I have is who placed that wooden box at the scene?

Japanese: もちろん、アクロさんです。
English: It was Mr. Dingling, of course.

Japanese: ロープの先に結びつけて、 窓から下ろすだけでいい。
English: He connected it to a rope, and then all he had to do was lower it down.

Japanese: いてッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: 言わせておけば‥‥この‥‥
English: Allow me to whip some sense into you!

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一がッ！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright!!

Japanese: いてててッ！ ‥‥いてッ！
English: OWWW!! OWWW!! OWWW!!

Japanese: 箱を持ち上げるとき、アタマの 位置にはバラつきがあるはずよ！
English: The Ringmaster's head could have been anywhere when he lifted the box!

Japanese: そのために、木箱には ある“細工”がされていたんです！
English: That's why the box was so specially made!
S-S-Specially made?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: Indeed!

Japanese: モチロン、それは‥‥
English: It had the most peculiar feature...

Japanese: 木箱の中身はなんでしたか！
English: Do you recall what was inside the wooden box?

Japanese: たしか、コショウの小ビンが 入っているだけ‥‥でしたね。
English: There was only a small bottle of pepper, wasn't there?

Japanese: それがなんだと言うの！
English: Get on with it!!
Well...

Japanese: なな、なぜそこで コトバにつまるッ！
English: What's the matter, cat got your tongue!?

Japanese: 木箱の中身とアタマの位置‥‥ なんのカンケイもないようですね。
English: I don't see any relationship between the location of his head and the box's contents.

Japanese: （ちょっと考えすぎたか‥‥）
English: (I think I might have outthought myself...)

Japanese: 考えなさすぎなの！
English: No Nick! You didn't think enough!!

Japanese: 弁護人。ホントに木箱には 細工など、されていたのですか？
English: Mr. Wright, do you still maintain the box had some sort of special feature?

Japanese: それは、木箱のサイズです！
English: The box was a remarkable size!

Japanese: サイズ‥‥？
English: Size...?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: Exactly!

Japanese: 写真を見ればわかるとおり、 木箱はかなり大きい。
English: I think you can tell from the picture that the wooden box was very large.

Japanese: 持ち上げるためには、 しゃがみこんで‥‥
English: To lift it up, one would have been required to squat...

Japanese: かかえこむしかない、 というわけですか‥‥。
English: Meaning that one would have had to have taken it up in both arms, right?

Japanese: しかも、この木箱は１０キロ。 けっこう重いですからね！
English: Moreover, the box is quite heavy! It weighs in the neighborhood of 20 pounds!

Japanese: それは、木箱の重さです！
English: The box has a remarkable weight!

Japanese: おもさ‥‥？
English: Weight...?

Japanese: 記録によれば、１０キロ‥‥ だったようですね。
English: According to the Court Record, it weighs 20 pounds...

Japanese: これだけの箱を持ち上げる としたら‥‥
English: Just to lift up this wooden box would have required...

Japanese: あ。ナルホド‥‥
English: Ohh... I see!

Japanese: しゃがんで、かかえこむしかない ‥‥というわけですね。
English: One would have to squat down, then lift it up with their body, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり！ 箱のサイズも大きめですしね。
English: That's exactly what I was trying to point out! The box is also very large.

Japanese: そういえば、箱の両側には 取っ手もついていましたな。
English: The box also has carrying handles on either side, doesn't it?

Japanese: これでおわかりでしょう！
English: That is correct!!

Japanese: 木箱を持ち上げるためには、 しゃがみこむ必要があった！
English: To lift up the box, you'd have to squat down!

Japanese: ‥‥それならば！
English: Which means...

Japanese: アタマの位置は、だれでも ほぼ、いっしょになるのです！
English: That no matter who you are, your head would be in approximately the same place!

Japanese: バカなァァァッ！
English: Fool!!

Japanese: （このヒト‥‥ ぼくの話、聞いてたのか‥‥？）
English: (Does she even bother to listen to me anymore...?)

Japanese: ‥‥聞いてましたよ。
English: I've heard what you've had to say...

Japanese: あなたのイマジネーションには ビックリしましたよ‥‥。
English: I must admit, I'm shocked at your imaginative skills.

Japanese: あ、あなたが‥‥置いたのですか？ この、木箱を！
English: Y-You... Did you drop it? Did you drop the bust onto the Ringmaster!?

Japanese: ナニを言ってるんですか。 そんなこと‥‥不可能ですよ。
English: What are you talking about!? Even if I had wanted to do such a thing, I couldn't.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥！
English: What...!?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん。この胸像が どこにあったか、ご存じですか？
English: Mr. Wright... Do you recall the original location of this bust?

Japanese: もちろん、知ってます。 食堂のテーブルの上‥‥
English: Of course I remember. It was on top of the table in the cafeteria.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ それが、どうかしましたか？
English: Hmm... Then what happened to it?

Japanese: ‥‥いやあ。ちょっと ど忘れしました。
English: Uhh... I kinda forget.

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん！ まさかまた、キオクソーシツ！
English: N-N-Nick!! Don't tell me you're losing your memory already!

Japanese: いやいやいや。そんな オオゲサな話じゃないけど‥‥
English: No no no no no... Let's not get carried away...

Japanese: アタマ出して！ 真宵ちゃん・キックを‥‥
English: Hey, stick out your head a little! I've got just the thing... A MAYA KICK!!

Japanese: コラコラ、そこ！ 弁護人をけらない。
English: Enough of that! No horseplay in this courtroom! And no kicking the defense attorney!!

Japanese: 胸像‥‥法廷記録には、 “食堂から消えた”とありますね。
English: According to the Court Record, the bust "disappeared from the cafeteria."

Japanese: ‥‥思い出してほしいんです。
English: I'd like you to remember one important fact, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: オレ、ひとりじゃ宿舎から 出られないんですよ？
English: I could not possibly leave the lodging house by myself.

Japanese: ‥‥あ！ そ、それって‥‥
English: Ah! That means...

Japanese: おわかりですか？
English: You understand what I mean, don't you?

Japanese: たしかに、胸像を落とすことは できたかもしれません。
English: I may very well have been able to drop the bust from my room.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、どうやって胸像を 食堂から部屋まで運んだんですか？
English: However, how would I have gotten the bust from the cafeteria to my room?

Japanese: そ、そのとおりよ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: You see, Mr. Phoenix Wright!! Explain that!

Japanese: “共犯者はいない” そう言ったのはあなたよ！
English: Don't forget... You said, "There was no accomplice!"

Japanese: ぐぐううッ！
English: Arrrggghhh!

Japanese: この証人が、どうやって 胸像を持ちこんだと言うの！
English: Tell us exactly how the witness would have carried the bust from the cafeteria!

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり、それが モンダイになるよな‥‥）
English: (Yep... We definitely have a problem here...)

Japanese: （しかし！　ここはもう、 迷っている場面じゃない！）
English: (But this is no place to get perplexed!)

Japanese: （つきつけるんだ！ ‥‥ホコリをかけて！）
English: (I've got to get my wits about me and prove how things happened once and for all!)

Japanese: では、うかがいましょう。
English: Alright, Mr. Wright. Let's hear your explanation.

Japanese: 胸像はどうやって、食堂から 証人の部屋へ移動したのですか？
English: How did the witness get the bust from the cafeteria back to his room?

Japanese: これがその、証拠です！
English: Here is the evidence that proves how he carried it!

Japanese: ‥‥どうですか？　狩魔検事。
English: What do you think about this evidence, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: ‥‥いかが？　証人。
English: What do you think about this evidence, Witness?

Japanese: ‥‥どうなんですか？ 成歩堂さん。
English: What do you think about this evidence, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ぎゃあッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: 私からも！
English: Here's one from me too!!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん・キック！
English: MAYA KICK!!

Japanese: ううううう‥‥ （凶器の胸像‥‥か‥‥）
English: Ow ow ow ow ow ow... (And here I thought the bust was the deadly weapon...)

Japanese: ‥‥“サル”‥‥ですか？
English: A monkey?

Japanese: ピカピカ光るものが大好きな、 おなじみ、ルーサーくんです。
English: Everyone knows Money... He loves shiny objects of any size.

Japanese: たしか、腹話術師のゆびわを 盗んだのでしたな‥‥
English: For instance, he stole the ventriloquist's ring...

Japanese: まさか‥‥証人がサルに 胸像を盗ませたというつもり？
English: So? Are you saying the witness had a monkey steal the bust?

Japanese: とんでもない！
English: Of course he didn't order the monkey to steal it!!

Japanese: 彼は、勝手に持ち帰ったんです。 ‥‥自分のウチにね。
English: The monkey stole it on his own and then brought it back home.

Japanese: “自分のウチ”‥‥？
English: "Home"...?

Japanese: ルーサーのウチは、 アクロさんの部屋にあるんです。
English: Money lives in Acro's room.

Japanese: あ‥‥アクロさんの！
English: ACRO'S ROOM!?

Japanese: 胸像は、ブロンズ製だったはずよ！ 光るワケが‥‥
English: But the bust was bronze, wasn't it? Bronze isn't all that shiny...

Japanese: たまにはムチを置いて、 法廷記録を見るべきですね！
English: Maybe you should put the whip down sometimes and read the Court Record!

Japanese: キレイな‥‥トランプですね。
English: My, those are some very nice cards he's holding.

Japanese: おそらく、プラチナでしょう。 ‥‥この上なくピカピカです！
English: Yes, and they are made of platinum... which is very shiny!

Japanese: うぐゥゥッ！
English: GWWWAAAAHHH!!

Japanese: アクロさん！
English: Acro!!

Japanese: たしかルーサーは、 チカラ持ちということでしたね！
English: Money is a strong monkey, right!?

Japanese: この胸像を持ち帰るぐらい、 カンタンだったのでは‥‥？
English: It'd be easy for him to bring the bust back to your room!!

Japanese: それぐらい持てなかったら、 オレの部屋から追い出しますよ。
English: If he wasn't able to handle that himself, I'd be on the market for a new roommate.

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にィィ！ ‥‥狩魔検事！
English: Order! Order! I SAID ORDER!! MS. VON KARMA!!

Japanese: モンダイの胸像は、いったい今 どこにあるんですか！
English: Where is the bust in question at this moment!?

Japanese: う‥‥ム。 それは‥‥わかっていないわ。
English: Umm... Umm... Umm... I... Umm... I don't know.

Japanese: ‥‥現在、捜査中‥‥よ！
English: We're searching for it as we speak...!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ちょっと フシギなのですが‥‥。
English: Hmm... This is a strange turn of events.

Japanese: もし、そのサルが胸像を 盗まなかったら‥‥、
English: If that monkey did not steal the bust...

Japanese: この事件は どうなっていたのでしょう？
English: Then what happens to this case?

Japanese: ‥‥そのときは‥‥
English: Well, in that event...

Japanese: きっと、他のモノが 凶器になったのでしょう。
English: Something else must have been used as the murder weapon.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥
English: Well... Hmm...

Japanese: この胸像が凶器になったの ‥‥グーゼン、ってコト？
English: Or maybe this bust was the murder weapon... But it was used by accident.

Japanese: たぶんね。
English: That's possible.

Japanese: ルーサーがたまたま盗んできたのを 見て“使える”と思ったんだ。
English: Maybe Acro saw Money's mountain of stolen goods and thought to use one of them.

Japanese: とにかく！
English: Anyways...

Japanese: “アクロさんには 犯行が可能だった”‥‥
English: I think we've more than proven one critical fact.

Japanese: これで、立証できたのでは ないでしょうか！
English: Namely, that it was entirely possible that Acro was the murderer!

Japanese: バカなああッ！
English: MORON!!

Japanese: 弁護人の主張はムチャクチャです！ しかし‥‥
English: Mr. Wright's argument was so circular, I'm still a bit dizzy! However...

Japanese: スジは通っています。 反証しようがない‥‥
English: His argument does hold water... There's no denying that.

Japanese: がはッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥まどわされてはダメ！ イカサマ弁護士の思うツボよ！
English: Don't seem so flamboozled! Especially by this fraud of an attorney!

Japanese: イカサマ‥‥
English: Fraud...?

Japanese: イチバン大事なコトを 忘れてるわ！　‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: You've forgotten the absolute most important thing, Mr. Phoenix Wright!!

Japanese: な‥‥なんのことだ？
English: And what is that?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょ！
English: You should know!!

Japanese: 現場で、あなたのイカサマ魔術師が 目撃されてるコトよ！
English: You forgot that your fraud of a magical client was spotted at the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ‥‥あああッ！
English: AHHHHH!!

Japanese: あのイカサマピエロの証言に、 ギモンの余地はなかったはず！
English: There is no reason to doubt the clown's testimony!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！ どうなんですかッ、成歩堂くん！
English: T-That's true! How do you respond to that, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥ここで負けちゃダメ！
English: Nick! Don't let her beat you now!

Japanese: （そうだ‥‥！　今こそ 逆転のチャンスをつかめ！）
English: (I won't! This is my chance to turn this trial around!)

Japanese: 殺人があったとき、 現場には２人の人物がいた！
English: When the murder occurred, there were two people at the scene of the crime!

Japanese: そう！　被害者の立見　七百人 ‥‥そして、犯人よ！
English: One was the victim, Russell Berry, and the other was the murderer himself!

Japanese: 今すぐ答えてもらうわ！ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Answer this and only this, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ピエロが見た、犯人の人影はッ！ いったい、だれだったと言うの！
English: The clown saw the murderer! Who was it then!?

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか！
English: What is this supposed to mean!?

Japanese: どういうこと！
English: Yes! What is that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: 説明してもらえますか？
English: Do you mind explaining that to me, as well?

Japanese: ‥‥質問ぜめだね、なるほどくん。
English: You're getting barraged with questions, Nick...

Japanese: どうしてかな？
English: Why is that?

Japanese: まちがってるからでしょ！
English: It's because you're on the wrong track!

Japanese: うう‥‥
English: Ohh...

Japanese: ‥‥コイツを見
English: He saw Max's B--

Japanese: うわわ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥聞いてるのは、もう１つの 人影が“だれ”だったか、よ。
English: I asked who was the other person Moe saw on the scene!

Japanese: ‥‥証拠品なんて、カンケイない！
English: That evidence has nothing to do with the question!

Japanese: ‥‥それこそが、 最後のポイントだったのです！
English: Au contraire mon frére! It does indeed have something to do with the question!

Japanese: トミーさんが見た “マックス”の影‥‥
English: Moe said that he saw Max's silhouette...

Japanese: しかしそれは、 人間ではなかった！
English: But he did not actually see the man himself! It wasn't a human being he saw!

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですかッ！
English: H-H-How is that possible!?

Japanese: カンタンなことです！
English: It's simple, really.

Japanese: トミーさんが目撃したのは ‥‥胸像だったのです！
English: What Moe actually saw that night was Max's bust!

Japanese: ななな、ナニをバ、バカなッ！
English: What are you talking about!? Have you tried using your brain at all in this case!?

Japanese: 目撃された“人影”は マントを着ていたのよ！
English: The silhouette he saw just happened to be wearing a cloak!

Japanese: マント！　そんなもの、 胸像にだって着せられる！
English: There's no reason why you couldn't attach a cloak to the bust!

Japanese: 広げたトランプに、マントは カンタンに引っかかるでしょう！
English: Or a cloak like that could easily get snagged on the bust if they came into contact.

Japanese: バカなことをッ！ いったいだれが着せたと言うの！
English: Idiot!! Who in their right mind would put a cloak on a bust!?

Japanese: そんなことは、 たいした問題じゃない！
English: It doesn't matter who put it on the bust!

Japanese: ちょちょ、ちょっと 待ちなさい弁護人！
English: Just wait a minute now, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: “胸像に、だれが マントを着せたか？”‥‥
English: Who put the cloak on the bust?

Japanese: ものすごく重要な問題では ないですかッ！
English: That question is of the utmost importance to this case, don't you agree!?

Japanese: （くそ！　バレたか‥‥）
English: (Doh! He caught me...)

Japanese: ‥‥あらためて 弁護人にうかがいましょう。
English: So let's have it Mr. Wright...

Japanese: “胸像にマントを着せた”‥‥ その人物はいったい、だれですか！
English: Who put the cloak on the bust?

Japanese: ‥‥ふう‥‥。 いいかしら？　成歩堂　龍一。
English: Hmm... I wonder, Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: あの夜の練習で、被告人は ずっとマントを着ていたのよ？
English: Didn't the defendant wear his cloak the entire time he was at practice?

Japanese: はあ。
English: Uhh...

Japanese: その人物がいつ、マントを 手に入れることができたというの？
English: So then how did this person get a hold of the cloak?
Well...

Japanese: それに。その人物はどうやって、 胸像にマントをかけたのかしら？
English: Furthermore, how would this person have gotten the cloak onto the bust, I wonder?

Japanese: 現場では、そんな人物は 目撃されていないのよ？
English: Because this person was not spotted at the scene...

Japanese: ‥‥ご、ごめんなさい。
English: I'm... I'm sorry.

Japanese: （やさしく言われると、 それはそれでシミるなあ‥‥）
English: (When she's so nice about telling me I'm wrong, it hurts even more...)

Japanese: あいて！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔流・“アメとムチ”‥‥
English: The famous Von Karma "carrot and whip" approach!

Japanese: バ‥‥バカなッ！ そ、そのオトコは‥‥
English: F-F-FOOL!! H-HIM...!?

Japanese: 被害者の立見　七百人 自身ではないですか！
English: You are saying it was the victim himself!? Russell Berry!?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: That's what I'm saying!

Japanese: 彼が‥‥被害者自身が マントを着せたというの！
English: He... I mean, the victim himself, placed the cloak on the bust!?

Japanese: “着せた”という言いかたは 正確ではありませんが‥‥。
English: "Placed the cloak" isn't really the right way of putting it...

Japanese: いったい、どういうコトですか ‥‥弁護人！
English: Then what would be the right way of putting it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 説明しなさい！
English: Explain yourself!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ゼンブ‥‥わかったの？
English: Nick! Do you really have a handle on all of this?

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。今までの 断片をつなげれば‥‥
English: I'm fine, Maya. I'm finally putting all the pieces together...

Japanese: できあがる“絵”は １つしかないからね。
English: There's really only one picture I can paint, anyways.

Japanese: ‥‥では。
English: Alright...

Japanese: あの夜、本当は何が起こったのか？ ここで再現してみましょう。
English: So you want to know what really happened that night? Let's step back in time.

Japanese: アクロさんは、ロープを使って 現場に木箱をセットします。
English: Acro used a rope to lower the wooden box onto the scene.

Japanese: そして、そのロープを胸像に むすびつけて‥‥
English: Then he attached that rope to the bust,

Japanese: 部屋の窓のそとにつるします。 木箱の真上に落ちるように‥‥。
English: and dangled the bust out of his bedroom window, directly above the wooden box.

Japanese: 一方、団長はマックスを部屋に 待たせて、現場に向かいます。
English: At the same time, the Ringmaster told Max to wait in his room, and went to the

Japanese: このとき、もちろん団長は マックスの衣装を身につけました。
English: scene. Of course, at the time, the Ringmaster was wearing Max's costume.

Japanese: おそらく、自分のすがたを 見られたくなかったのでしょう。
English: Perhaps he didn't want anyone to recognize him that night.

Japanese: シンパイしたとおり、彼は宿舎の 入り口で目撃されてしまいます。
English: But just as he feared, he was spotted at the entrance of the lodging house.

Japanese: ‥‥ベンさんと リロくんによって。
English: By none other than a ventriloquist and his puppet, Ben and Trilo.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、団長は現場に到着。 足下の木箱を持ち上げようとする！
English: When the Ringmaster arrived at the scene, he bent over to lift the wooden box.

Japanese: それを見はからって、アクロさんは ロープを解きはなつ！
English: And that's when Acro took his chance and released the rope!

Japanese: そして‥‥ここから、 いよいよ大魔術の始まりです。
English: Now this is when the magic happens...

Japanese: 被害者に胸像が命中した、 その瞬間‥‥！
English: At the very instant that the bust hit the victim...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！ 待ちなさい成歩堂　龍一！
English: You wait just a second there, Mr. Phoenix Wright!!

Japanese: い、いくらなんでも‥‥
English: As much as you try...

Japanese: いくらなんでも コレはないでしょ！　コレはッ！
English: As much as you scheme... This just isn't true! It can't be!

Japanese: ‥‥オドロクのは まだ早いよ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: It's still a little early to be getting so upset, Ms. von Karma...

Japanese: サーカスはまだ、終わらない。
English: This circus isn't over yet.

Japanese: 殴られたショックで、 マントは胸像に引っかかった。
English: With the shock of impact, it threw up the cloak and it got snagged onto the bust.

Japanese: ‥‥このときの音を聞いて、 窓のそとを見た人物がいました。
English: That's when the sound was heard by a witness and he took a look out of his window.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。 ピエロのトミーさんです。
English: That witness was, of course, Lawrence "Moe" Curls, the clown.

Japanese: トミーさんが目撃したとき、すでに 胸像には、マントがかかっていた。
English: When Moe looked out his window, the cloak had already snagged onto the bust.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Now...

Japanese: 犯行を終えたアクロさんは当然、 凶器の回収にかかります。
English: Having completed the crime, Acro naturally went about pulling up the murder weapon.

Japanese: モチロン彼は、胸像のマントも、 トミーさんが見てるのも知らない。
English: Of course, he had no idea that Moe saw the bust being raised with the cloak dangling on it.

Japanese: 車イスに座っているため、窓の下を 見ることができないからです。
English: Primarily because, in his wheelchair, he couldn't see out of his window.

Japanese: ‥‥彼は、胸像に結びつけてある ロープを引いていきます。
English: So he just kept pulling the bust up.

Japanese: “空に消えた殺人犯”。 これが、大魔術のタネ明かしです。
English: And that is how the "magical" murderer disappearing into the sky came to be.

Japanese: この犯行を行うことが できたのは‥‥
English: Now you know how the murder actually took place...

Japanese: 現場の真上から、凶器を落とす ことができた人物！
English: And now you know who was able to drop the murder weapon from above the scene!

Japanese: アクロさん！ ‥‥あなたしかいない！
English: Acro! It could only have been you!

Japanese: アクロさん、 コトリとたわむれてるよ。
English: Acro's been playing mind games with all of us!
(He sure has...)

Japanese: （今までの、ぼくの スイリはなんだったんだ‥‥）
English: (But he has come to the end of his rope now.)

Japanese: それで‥‥？
なんだと‥‥！
English: What now!?

Japanese: あなたは長い “物語”を語った‥‥
English: You've graced us with a rather long-winded tale...

Japanese: でも、それが“真実”だった という‥‥証拠はあるのかしら？
English: But do you have any evidence to prove that your fairy tale is true!?

Japanese: しょ‥‥しょうこ‥‥
English: E-Evidence...?

Japanese: 裁きの庭で発言力を持つのは 証拠品と、このムチ‥‥
English: In this court, only two things matter. The power of evidence, and the power of my whip.

Japanese: そして、もちろん この木槌‥‥ですね。
English: Don't forget the power of my gavel as well!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 狩魔検事の言うとおりです。
English: Mr. Wright, the prosecution brings up a good point. Can we see some evidence?

Japanese: でも、なるほどくん！ 証拠、って言われても‥‥
English: Nick... They say they want evidence...

Japanese: ‥‥犯行はまちがいなく、 今、説明した方法で行われた。
English: I just explained how there can only be one possible murder method.

Japanese: だから、トミーさんの目撃談には 不自然な点があったんだよ。
English: But there is still something unusual about Moe's eye- witness account.

Japanese: “不自然な点”‥‥？
English: "Unusual"?

Japanese: ムジュン、ってヤツさ。
English: A contradiction, actually.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ！ それをつきつけてやれば‥‥！
English: OK then! Use that and get out of this jam!

Japanese: ナイショ話はそこまでです。 ‥‥弁護人。
English: That's enough talking amongst yourselves. Proceed, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 証拠を提示して いただきましょう。
English: Present some evidence to the court that backs your claims.

Japanese: 大魔術の“タネ明かし”が “真実”であった証拠を‥‥！
English: I want hard proof that you have unraveled the trick to this magic case...!

Japanese: やりなおし。
English: Try again.

Japanese: （あと２秒チンモクがつづいて いたら、叫びだすトコだった）
English: (If they would have been silent for two more seconds, I would have screamed!)

Japanese: ‥‥問題は‥‥ マックスの３つのシンボルです。
English: The problem is Max's three symbols...

Japanese: シルクハットと、マントと、 白いバラ‥‥。
English: You know... The silk hat, the cloak, and the white roses.

Japanese: きのうの法廷でも、 何度も問題になりましたね。
English: Those symbols were a problem numerous times during yesterday's proceedings.

Japanese: トミーさんの証言には、 ２つのムジュンがありました。
English: Yesterday, there were two contradictions in Moe's testimony.

Japanese: シルクハットのムジュンと、 白いバラのムジュン‥‥。
English: The silk hat was one. The white roses were the other.

Japanese: さっきのタネ明かしならば、 すべてのムジュンが解決します！
English: But the theory I just presented explains all of these contradictions!

Japanese: な‥‥何をバカな‥‥！
English: You fool! Do you ever shut up!?

Japanese: 現場からは、マックスの シルクハットが発見されています。
English: Max's silk hat was found at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: しかし！　トミーさんは きのう、ハッキリと証言した！
English: However! Remember what Moe said yesterday.

Japanese: 『現場から飛び去った犯人は、 シルクハットをかぶっていた』と！
English: He testified that the criminal he saw fleeing the scene was wearing a silk hat!

Japanese: 考えられることは１つ！
English: There's only one explanation for that!

Japanese: トミーさんが見た“シルクハット” ‥‥それは、胸像だったのです！
English: The "silk hat" that Moe saw was actually the bust!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥そう考えれば、 ムジュンではなくなります！
English: Makes sense... If you look at it that way, then he did see the silk hat, well, sort of.

Japanese: そ、それならば、もう１つの ムジュンはどうなるの！
English: Fine, you've got one, but what about the other contradiction!?

Japanese: もう１つの‥‥？
English: The other contradiction...?

Japanese: あの腹話術師の 証言を思い出して！
English: Remember what that ventriloquist said in court!

Japanese: 彼らは、“マックス”の ムネに、白いバラを目撃している！
English: He said that he witnessed "white roses" on "Max's" chest that night!

Japanese: でも、ピエロの証言はちがった！
English: But the clown's testimony doesn't match!

Japanese: ピエロは、白いバラは “なかった”と証言している！
English: The clown said that there were no "white roses"!

Japanese: このムジュンも 説明できると言うの！
English: I'd like to see you try and explain that one away!!

Japanese: できるの？　なるほどくん！
English: Can you do it, Nick!?

Japanese: ‥‥モチロン！ すべてに説明がつきます。
English: Of course! I can explain all of it!

Japanese: なんですって‥‥！
English: What was that...!?

Japanese: もう一度、胸像にマントが かかる瞬間を思い出してください！
English: Please recall the instant when the cloak snagged onto the bust!

Japanese: 胸像にマントがかかると、 白いバラは、どうなるか？
English: If the cloak snagged onto the bust, what happened to the white roses?

Japanese: ‥‥おわかりでしょうか。 マントが前から胸像にかかると‥‥
English: Do you get it yet? If the cloak got snagged onto the front of the bust...

Japanese: 白いバラは、背中側に まわってしまうのです！
English: It means that the white roses would end up on the back of the bust!

Japanese: あああああッ！
English: AHHHHH!!

Japanese: だからこそ！ トミーさんには見えなかった！
English: Which explains why Moe didn't see it!

Japanese: 白いバラは、胸像の 背中側に消えていたのです！
English: The white roses were not visible because they were on the back side of the bust!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にィィィッ！
English: Order! Orderrrrrrrr!

Japanese: ‥‥おどろくべき事態です。
English: This is quite the shocking state of affairs.

Japanese: 弁護人のスイリは、またしても ムチャであるように聞こえます。
English: Mr. Wright's theory still sounds a bit absurd to me.

Japanese: しかし‥‥これまた スジが通っている！
English: However... Let's just keep going down this road for a while and see where it leads!

Japanese: よしッ！ これで、カルマ検事も‥‥
English: Let's do this, Nick! Then maybe Von Karma will finally throw in the towel...

Japanese: ま、まだヨユウみたいだね。
English: Well... So much for that theory.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。ちょっと よろしいでしょうか。
English: Mr. Wright... Do you mind?

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: オレたちのサーカスのコト、 ちゃんと調べてくれましたよね？
English: You took the time to research our circus, didn't you?

Japanese: え、ええ。 そのつもりですけど‥‥。
English: Well... Yes, I did... Is there something making you think that I didn't?

Japanese: だったら、わかって もらえると思うんですけど。
English: If you did, then maybe you'll understand why I think you're off track.

Japanese: あの。‥‥何を‥‥？
English: Umm... Why is that?

Japanese: 動機よ。
English: Motive.

Japanese: この証人は、立見団長に 大きな恩を感じている。
English: This witness feels an incredible debt of gratitude towards the Ringmaster.

Japanese: サーカスの関係者ならば、 だれでも知っているわ。
English: Anyone with any relation to the circus is well aware of this.

Japanese: そんな証人が、団長を 殺害するなど‥‥あり得ないわ！
English: Thus, there is absolutely no way someone like this would kill the Ringmaster!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 裁判長。アクロ氏の話を 聞いていただきたい！
English: Your Honor. I'd like you to hear Acro's story!

Japanese: ‥‥立見　七百人団長との関係、 これまでの生活について。
English: Learn about his relationship with the Ringmaster, and his life up until now...

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしよう‥‥！
English: W-What do we do!?

Japanese: たしかにアクロさん、団長さんの コト、すごく尊敬してたもん‥‥
English: There's no doubting that Acro deeply respected the Ringmaster!

Japanese: （アクロさんの動機‥‥か‥‥）
English: (Acro's motive... Hmm...)

Japanese: どうやら、事件はまだ 終わらないようですね‥‥。
English: It seems that this case isn't over yet...

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥いったん、 ここで休憩をとりましょう。
English: Very well. However, I feel this is a good place to take a break.

Japanese: アクロさんの証言は、 その後、聞かせてもらいます。
English: I will listen to the rest of Mr. Dingling's testimony after recess.

Japanese: では、これより １０分間の休憩に入ります！
English: This court will now take a 10 minute recess.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、山田　耕平に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Billy Bob Johns...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
